note: The succession-drill commission, structured for instruments/conformance_check.py (ceremony component 1 of the CLAUDE.md succession rule). Each scope item is one drill deliverable; the report beside this file carries the verdicts and witnesses. file/commit witnesses are verified mechanically; ledger_row/doc_excerpt witnesses print a loud OPERATOR-CHECK line (the checker cannot reach them and must not silently pass them).

scope.mandate: Author design/SPEC-DOC-ATTESTATION-2.md: ADR-0017-legible, with an ADR-0000 closure statement enumerating the record-shape universe.
scope.witness_hint: the spec exists at its committed path

scope.mandate: Implement doc-attestation/2 in the gate: schema dispatch, the adjudication validator bound to escalated, --record writing /2.
Extend the seen-red fixture with /2 cases and bank its both-polarity red evidence (valid /2 passes; escalated-without-adjudication and adjudication-without-escalation refused; /1 still valid).
Run the spec through the ADR-0017 A:B:C loop (synchronous B) and record its attestation in the ledger as a /2 record.
Adversarial fresh-context review of the spec by a second instance charged to refute; every finding repaired or rebutted on the record.
The closure statement checked against the enumerated universe by a third fresh instance; verdict banked.
Dated BACKLOG entry recording the drill AS a drill, pointing at all four ceremony artifacts.
